message:
test(patterns): add near-miss corpus for claude_code.model.selected (ft-fk3rn)

The claude_code.model.selected rule at patterns.rs:1907-1921 uses broad
anchors (`model:`, `Using model`, `claude-`) but previously had only
positive corpus fixtures (model_selected.txt + model_selected_v2.txt).
Add a negative pair to fence against false positives on generic docs
and non-Claude `Using model:` lines — same approach as ft-u4m4z /
ft-8ku8p.

Verified locally (Python regex equivalent): every anchor-matching line
in the fixture fails the regex because the captured model group
requires `claude-(opus|sonnet|haiku)-...`:

  L3 "Using model: gpt-4o-mini"          → anchor hit, regex fails (gpt-, not claude-)
  L4 "Set `model:` in your local ..."    → anchor hit, regex fails (backtick, not claude-)

Global re.search returns None; expect.json is [].

Fixture created by another pane; adopted as-is after verification.

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+Configuration reference:
+
+Using model: gpt-4o-mini
+Set `model:` in your local tool config if you want a different provider.
+
+This is documentation text, not a live Claude Code status line.
